ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee record */,
  "card_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the employee's card (training activity) */,
  "active_skill_set_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the active skill set associated with the employee */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the employee record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the employee record was last updated */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Employee's current job title */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the employee belongs to */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the manager of the employee */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the employee record */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the employee record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_active_skill_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("active_skill_set_id") REFERENCES skill_sets ("skill_set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "skill_sets" (
  "skill_set_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each skill set */,
  "skill_set_name" TEXT /* Name of the skill set */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the skill set, detailing its relevance */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the skill set (e.g., technical, soft skills) */,
  "skill_set_level" TEXT /* Level of expertise required for the skill set */,
  "description_long" TEXT /* Detailed description of the skill set */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the skill set was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the skill set was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("skill_set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "training_courses" (
  "course_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training course */,
  "course_name" TEXT /* Name of the training course */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the course in hours */,
  "skill_set_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the skill set that the course aims to develop */,
  "course_type" TEXT /* Type of the training course (e.g., online, in-person) */,
  "course_provider" TEXT /* Provider of the training course */,
  "skills_covered" TEXT /* Skills covered by the course */,
  "prerequisites" TEXT /* Prerequisites for taking the course */,
  "evaluation_method" TEXT /* Method of evaluation for the course */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("course_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_training_courses_skill_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("skill_set_id") REFERENCES skill_sets ("skill_set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_training" (
  "training_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the employee who completed the training */,
  "course_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the training course completed */,
  "completion_date" TEXT /* Date on which the training was completed */,
  "training_status" TEXT /* Status of the training (e.g., completed, in-progress) */,
  "evaluation_score" INTEGER /* Score obtained by the employee in the training */,
  "feedback" TEXT /* Feedback provided by the employee on the training */,
  "recommended" INTEGER /* Whether the employee recommends the training */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("training_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_training_course_id FOREIGN KEY ("course_id") REFERENCES training_courses ("course_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_training_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "skill_levels" (
  "skill_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each skill level */,
  "skill_level_name" TEXT /* Name of the skill level */,
  "skill_level_description" TEXT /* Description of the skill level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("skill_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_skill_levels" (
  "employee_skill_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee skill level record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the employee */,
  "skill_set_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the skill set */,
  "skill_level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the current skill level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_skill_level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_skill_levels_skill_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("skill_set_id") REFERENCES skill_sets ("skill_set_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_skill_levels_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_skill_levels_skill_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("skill_level_id") REFERENCES skill_levels ("skill_level_id")
)
